output: Implemented the approved plan in the repository and captured execution evidence.
commands_run: rg -n "useLocation|GlobalHeader|SocialIcons|container mx-auto|GlobalProcessingWarning|searchParams|pathname === '/tools/video-editor'|pathname === \"/tools/video-editor\"" src/app src/features/editor src/tools/video-editor src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts
deviations: Browser-level manual interaction checks for T3, T8, and T12 were not executable from this terminal-only session; validation was limited to code inspection, targeted search, and repeated `npm run build` passes.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `useVideoEditorRouteState` and used it in `src/app/Layout.tsx` and `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx` so full-screen chrome suppression only activates on `/tools/video-editor` when `?timeline=` is present. `Layout` now disables the split-view wrapper and hides `SocialIcons` in shell mode; `LayoutMainContent` suppresses `GlobalHeader`, `GlobalProcessingWarning`, container padding, pane offsets, and the shared header slot only in shell mode. `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx` now hides the pane controls for any video-editor route via the shared route helper.
commands_run: rg -n "useLocation|GlobalHeader|SocialIcons|container mx-auto|GlobalProcessingWarning|searchParams|pathname === '/tools/video-editor'|pathname === \"/tools/video-editor\"" src/app src/features/editor src/tools/video-editor src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx` to wrap the active editor shell in `h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden`, passed timeline names through `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and added a minimal back bar plus timeline title in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx`. The full editor grid now runs inside an `h-full flex-1` shell so it consumes the viewport made available by T1.

Ran `npm run build` after the layout/shell changes and it passed. Route restoration and dot suppression were verified by code path inspection only in this session: the layout gating is isolated behind `useVideoEditorRouteState`, and `EditorPaneTab` now null-returns on the editor route. Interactive browser confirmation was not available from the terminal.

Resolved the open variant-ID source by changing `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts` to select `generations.primary_variant_id` plus the joined `primary_variant` location/thumbnail, then surfaced that through `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/types.ts`. `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx` now drags the primary variant ID and a media-kind flag in `GenerationDropData`, and the dragged URL is the primary variant location rather than the thumbnail path.

Extended `AssetRegistryEntry` with `generationId` and `variantId` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts`, added `registerAsset` to `src/tools/video-editor/data/DataProvider.ts`, and implemented it in `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts` as a read-merge-write of the `asset_registry` JSONB column. `uploadAsset()` now generates IDs with the shared UUID fallback and delegates the registry write to `registerAsset` so uploads still persist end-to-end.

Added a provider-backed `registerAsset` wrapper in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts`, threaded it through `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts`, and extended `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts` so timeline dragover/drop now handles `application/x-generation` alongside files and asset-key drags. Generation drops create a local registry entry with `generateUUID()`, persist it through `registerAsset`, and then reuse `handleAssetDrop` to create the clip without disturbing file upload or asset-key paths.

`src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx` is now a generation drop target. It detects generation drags with the shared DnD helpers, shows an accent ring highlight on dragover, calls `registerGenerationAsset()` on drop, and intentionally does not create a timeline clip. The existing asset-key drag behavior for timeline drops remains intact.

Validated the DnD integration via build plus code inspection. The generation drag protocol remains the same shared `application/x-generation` helper, only extended with optional variant fields, so shot-drop consumers continue receiving the same base shape. Persistence now goes through `registerAsset`, which writes `asset_registry`, but browser-driven drag/reload checks were not run in this terminal session.
Changed `assetsExpanded` to default closed in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx` while keeping the existing toggle button behavior unchanged.

Added source click-through in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx`. Asset rows with `generationId` now fetch the source generation, disable the external-link control when that lookup returns null, and open `MediaLightbox` with `initialVariantId` from the registry entry so the originating variant is selected.

Replaced hardcoded `zinc-*` surfaces in the video editor component tree with theme tokens across shell, preview, timeline, compact mode, properties panels, and custom effect editor components. Verified with `rg` that `src/tools/video-editor/components` no longer contains `zinc-*`, `gray-*`, or `slate-*` classes.

Ran a final `npm run build` after the integration changes and it passed. The full-screen layout, persisted generation-asset registration, source lightbox wiring, and back navigation now connect through the runtime/context code paths. End-to-end browser interaction for navigate → drag → clip → click-through → navigate back was not executed from this terminal, so that portion remains code-inspected rather than manually exercised.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Fullscreen gating now uses `useVideoEditorRouteState` and requires both the video-editor route and `searchParams.has('timeline')`; list view keeps the normal header/footer/layout path.
`VideoEditorPage` wraps the active shell in `h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden`, and `VideoEditorShell` adds a 40px back bar with the runtime timeline name plus an `h-full` editor grid.
`npm run build` passed after the layout changes. Route restoration is isolated by the shared shell-route gate; browser navigation away/back was not manually exercised in this terminal.

The variant ID did not exist on the old generation item shape, so it is now sourced from `generations.primary_variant_id` via the updated `useProjectGenerations` select and `transformGeneration`. The dragged asset URL is the primary variant location, not the thumbnail.

`SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset()` performs the load-merge-write for `asset_registry`, and `uploadAsset()` now calls it internally after upload so the previous upload flow still persists registry updates.
`onTimelineDrop` now branches in order: Files, then generation drag data, then asset-key. `onTimelineDragOver` accepts generation/file/asset-key drags without collapsing those paths into one another.
`AssetPanel` shows an accent ring during generation dragover and calls `registerGenerationAsset()` on drop without invoking `handleAssetDrop`, so it registers assets without creating clips.

Dropped generation assets now persist through the new `registerAsset` DB write, and the existing generation-to-shot drag protocol stays on the same shared helper with extra optional fields only. Browser reload and drag regression checks were not manually run here.
`assetsExpanded` now initializes to `false` in `PropertiesPanel`, and the existing disclosure button still toggles the panel open/closed.
Asset rows query their source generation, disable the source button when that query returns null, and open `MediaLightbox` with `initialVariantId` from the registry entry when available.

A post-change `rg -n "zinc-|gray-|slate-" src/tools/video-editor/components src/tools/video-editor/pages` only returned a `bg-sky-400` handle in `OverlayEditor`; there are no remaining `zinc-*`, `gray-*`, or `slate-*` classes under the video-editor components tree.

The integrated code paths for full-screen layout, generation drag registration, source lightbox opening, and back navigation are wired together and the final `npm run build` passed. Full browser-driven end-to-end interaction was not executed from this terminal.
